verbatim_quote: "There was one knight," said Meera, "in the year of the false spring. The Knight of the Laughing Tree, they called him. He might have been a crannogman, that one."
note: Meera introduces the story as a tale she tells, setting up the context

verbatim_quote: The king himself was there, with his son the dragon prince. The White Swords had come, to welcome a new brother to their ranks. The storm lord was on hand, and the rose lord as well. The great lion of the rock had quarreled with the king and stayed away, but many of his bannermen and knights attended all the same.
note: Directly matches beats B02 description: king (Aerys), dragon prince (Rhaegar), White Swords (Kingsguard+Jaime), storm lord (Robert Baratheon), rose lord (Mace Tyrell), great lion (Tywin absent)

verbatim_quote: The great lion of the rock had quarreled with the king and stayed away, but many of his bannermen and knights attended all the same.
note: Tywin didn't attend the tourney; Meera's story explicitly shows Tywin quarreled with the king and stayed away while his bannermen attended. Connects to Tywin's anger over Jaime joining Kingsguard (ASOS Tyrion III)

verbatim_quote: They snatched away his spear and knocked him to the ground, cursing him for a frogeater.
note: The three squires attack the crannogman and mock him as frogeater. Line 183 follows: Lyanna rescues him

verbatim_quote: "Two," said Meera. "The she-wolf laid into the squires with a tourney sword, scattering them all. The crannogman was bruised and bloodied, so she took him back to her lair to clean his cuts and bind them up with linen. There he met her pack brothers: the wild wolf who led them, the quiet wolf beside him, and the pup who was youngest of the four."
note: The four Starks identified: she-wolf (Lyanna), wild wolf (Brandon), quiet wolf (Ned), pup (Benjen)

verbatim_quote: Amidst all this merriment, the little crannogman spied the three squires who'd attacked him. One served a pitchfork knight, one a porcupine, while the last attended a knight with two towers on his surcoat, a sigil all crannogmen know well.
note: Later at the feast, Howland spots the three squires and Meera identifies their knights

verbatim_quote: The lad was no knight, no more than any of his people. We sit a boat more often than a horse, and our hands are made for oars, not lances. Much as he wished to have his vengeance, he feared he would only make a fool of himself and shame his people.
note: Howland's doubts about competing. Contrasts with the Stark offer to help him tourney (next beat)

verbatim_quote: The quiet wolf had offered the little crannogman a place in his tent that night, but before he slept he knelt on the lakeshore, looking across the water to where the Isle of Faces would be, and said a prayer to the old gods of north and Neck . . .
note: Howland prays to the old gods by the lakeshore after Ned offers him shelter

verbatim_quote: No one knew," said Meera, "but the mystery knight was short of stature, and clad in ill-fitting armor made up of bits and pieces. The device upon his shield was a heart tree of the old gods, a white weirwood with a laughing red face.
note: Describes the mystery knight's armor and distinctive shield; line 225 follows with him defeating the three knights

verbatim_quote: That night at the great castle, the storm lord and the knight of skulls and kisses each swore they would unmask him, and the king himself urged men to challenge him, declaring that the face behind that helm was no friend of his. But the next morning, when the heralds blew their trumpets and the king took his seat, only two champions appeared. The Knight of the Laughing Tree had vanished. The king was wroth, and even sent his son the dragon prince to seek the man, but all they ever found was his painted shield, hanging abandoned in a tree.
note: Aerys sent Rhaegar to find the knight, but they found only the shield

verbatim_quote: No one knew," said Meera, "but the mystery knight was short of stature, and clad in ill-fitting armor made up of bits and pieces.
note: Describes the knight as short and wearing ill-fitting armor made from scraps
ASX's meta-theory argument about ASOIAF's narrative structure and the series' rules of magic. This is ASX's analytical commentary, not a grounded book beat. Not present in chapters
ASX's meta-analysis about mystery-resolution patterns in ASOIAF. This is interpretive commentary, not a direct textual beat from the books

verbatim_quote: "Are you certain you never heard this tale before, Bran?" asked Jojen. "Your lord father never told it to you?"
note: Jojen's question implies Ned knew or should have known the story, suggesting Ned may have direct knowledge of the knight's identity

Assertion that Brandon, Ned, and Benjen were all trained riders/fighters. While heavily implied by their status as Stark princes and references to Ned's training, no single passage explicitly lists all three as trained in riding/fighting specifically. Would require composite citation

verbatim_quote: "Brandon," she said sadly. "Brandon was wild, and he was proud. He did not like to hear no for an answer.
note: Catelyn describes Brandon as wild and proud—matches the paraphrase of 'hot-blooded' and 'gallant fool' personality

verbatim_quote: He remembered Brandon's laughter, and Robert's berserk valor in the melee, the way he laughed as he unhorsed men left and right. He remembered Jaime Lannister, a golden youth in scaled white armor, kneeling on the grass in front of the king's pavilion and making his vows to protect and defend King Aerys. Afterward, Ser Oswell Whent helped Jaime to his feet, and the White Bull himself, Lord Commander Ser Gerold Hightower, fastened the snowy cloak of the Kingsguard about his shoulders.
note: Reference to Brandon at Harrenhal tourney where he is not described as particularly short; contradicts 'short of stature' knight description
The claim that Brandon joust openly as himself after the mystery knight disappearance isn't directly sourced in Meera's story or the Harrenhal flashback. Would need external chapter reference

Speculation that Ned was younger/shorter than Brandon. While the books refer to Ned as 'shorter than Brandon' in appearance (acok-catelyn-05), a direct statement comparing their heights at Harrenhal doesn't appear
Theory that Ned riding for Howland explains their lifelong bond. While they are close (Howland goes with Ned to Tower of Joy), the causality is not established in book text

Claim that Ned has 'a certain distaste for the pageantry of tourneys.' While Ned is repeatedly described as solemn/somber (acok-catelyn-05 'so somber'), the specific phrase about distaste for tourney pageantry does not appear in the books

That Benjen at fourteen was 'the one who offered Howland armor and a horse.' While Meera's story says 'the pup offered' armor and horse, confirming this was the youngest brother, explicit confirmation that Benjen was fourteen at Harrenhal requires cross-reference not provided in Meera's narrative
Claims Benjen at fourteen was 'very young' with no suggestion of fighting/riding skill. No explicit statement in the books denies Benjen's martial skill at fourteen; this is an inference

That Lyanna 'could fit the Knight's short stature and ill-fitting armor.' While Lyanna is described as wilful and boyish, no explicit passage confirms her height at fifteen or ability to wear 'ill-fitting armor'

verbatim_quote: That's my father's man you're kicking," howled the she-wolf.
note: Lyanna rescues Howland from the squires, establishing clear motivation for helping him win back honor
Quote 'wild' describing Lyanna appears in video/fan commentary but specific textual cite to 'wild' + 'willful' + 'boyish' in direct Lyanna passage not located in sources/chapters/

Speculation about helmet disguising voice (booming sound from echo). The 'booming' description is present (line 225), but the theory that this masks a woman's voice is interpretive, not textually grounded
Assertion about whether a fifteen-year-old girl would have strength to use a lance. No book passage directly addresses the physical feasibility for Lyanna specifically

verbatim_quote: A great jouster must be a great horseman first.
note: Jaime's observation that jousting ability requires horsemanship skill, supporting ASX's argument that Lyanna's renowned riding would translate to jousting ability

verbatim_quote: The king was wroth, and even sent his son the dragon prince to seek the man, but all they ever found was his painted shield, hanging abandoned in a tree. It was the dragon prince who won that tourney in the end.
note: Rhaegar was sent to unmask the knight, then later crowned Lyanna queen of love and beauty and ran off with her—supports ASX's theory about Rhaegar possibly finding and being impressed by the mystery knight
